# UX audit — RavenClaude dashboard (`plugins/ravenclaude-core/dashboard.html`)

**Author:** UX/product review · **Date:** 2026-05-30 · **Status:** specification (no code changed)

**Scope.** This audits the **user-facing control surface** generated by
[`scripts/generate-dashboards.py`](../scripts/generate-dashboards.py) into
`plugins/ravenclaude-core/dashboard.html` and served by
[`scripts/serve-dashboards.py`](../scripts/serve-dashboards.py). It is distinct from
`repo-guide.html` (public website content). The dashboard is where a user **configures
their posture, watches the guardrail pipeline, runs the install bridge, and reaches the
slash commands** for their own environment + agents.

This is a **specification**, not an implementation. Every recommendation is grounded in
the current code (function names + line-anchored behavior). Where a recommendation bumps
against a real technical limit, it is called out under **Capability Grounding** rather than
hand-waved.

---

## 0. How the dashboard is built (ground truth)

`render_dashboard()` ([generate-dashboards.py:116](../scripts/generate-dashboards.py))
composes one page from these tab renderers, in tab-bar order
([tab bar at :6742](../scripts/generate-dashboards.py)):

| Order | Tab label (tab-bar) | Renderer | `data-tab` |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Settings | `_render_settings_tab` (:903) | `settings` |
| 2 | Pipeline | `_render_pipeline_tab` (:361) | `pipeline` |
| 3 | Install & Update | `_render_install_tab` (:887) | `install` |
| 4 | Test a command | `_render_simulator_tab` (:150) | `simulator` |
| 5 | Learn | `_render_learn_tab` (:564) | `learn` |
| 6 | ⚖ Review log | `_render_saga_tab` (:173) | `saga` |
| 7 | Commands | `_render_commands_tab` (:728) | `commands` |
| 8 | Guidance | `_render_trees_tab` (:820) | `trees` |
| 9 | Activity | `_render_activity_tab` (:183) | `activity` |

**Served-vs-static detection.** Interactive tabs probe the local server with a `HEAD`
request before enabling live controls: `HEAD /__save` (Settings), `HEAD /__run` (Install),
`HEAD /__classify` (Test a command), `HEAD /__saga` / `HEAD /__runs` (Review log / Activity)
— see `do_HEAD` ([serve-dashboards.py:310](../scripts/serve-dashboards.py)). On a static
host (GitHub Pages / `file://` / `python3 -m http.server`) those probes 404 and the tab
degrades to copy-to-clipboard + help text. This "light up only when served" idiom is the
backbone of the whole interactivity model and must be preserved.

**Security posture (must be respected by every recommendation):**

- The server binds `127.0.0.1` by default (`0.0.0.0` only inside a Codespace so the port-forwarder can reach it) — [serve-dashboards.py:728/740](../scripts/serve-dashboards.py).
- Every state-changing endpoint is **Origin/Host CSRF-guarded** against `_ALLOWED_ORIGINS` / `_ALLOWED_HOSTS` ([:226–232](../scripts/serve-dashboards.py), enforced in `do_GET`/`do_POST`).
- `/__run` executes **only** the fixed action set `ALLOWED_ACTIONS = {"install", "update", "status"}` ([:83](../scripts/serve-dashboards.py)), each mapped to a fixed `bash scripts/ravenclaude <action>` argv with **no caller-supplied args or shell** (`_handle_run` :612–647). `/__save` and `/__read` are likewise allow-listed to fixed config paths.
- **This allow-list discipline is non-negotiable.** Any widening proposed below stays an allow-list — never an arbitrary-command surface.

---

## 1. Purpose-communication gap

### Finding

A first-time user landing on the dashboard sees the **Settings** tab first (it has
`class="tab-panel active"`, [:6756](../scripts/generate-dashboards.py)) — a dense grid of
per-category file/network/shell/package autonomy radios. **There is no Overview / Home /
"Start here" surface anywhere in the nine tabs.** Nothing on first paint answers:

- *What is RavenClaude?* (a private Claude Code plugin marketplace — per [`README.md`](../README.md) and [`docs/architecture.md`](../docs/architecture.md))
- *What does it contain?* (13 plugins; `ravenclaude-core` = neutral team of 14 agents + 22 skills + 11 hooks + the tribunal; 12 domain plugins)
- *What is this dashboard for vs. `repo-guide.html`?*
- *What should I do first?*

The user is dropped straight into the most advanced surface (per-permission autonomy tuning)
with no framing. Every tab assumes the user already knows the mental model
(posture → pipeline → tribunal → bridge).

### Recommendation — add an **Overview** tab, make it the default

Add `_render_overview_tab()` and register it **first** in the tab bar (new
`data-tab="overview"`), moving the `active` class onto it. Settings becomes tab 2. This is
purely additive — no existing tab loses content (see §5).

The Overview tab should contain, top to bottom:

| Block | Content | Source of truth (so it doesn't drift) |
|---|---|---|
| **What this is** | One paragraph: "RavenClaude is your private Claude Code plugin marketplace. This dashboard is *your control surface* — it tunes how autonomous your agents are, shows the guardrails every action passes through, and wires the plugin into your tools." Explicitly distinguish from `repo-guide.html` ("the public catalog of agents/skills"). | `README.md` line 3–9, `docs/architecture.md` §"The marketplace model" |
| **What's installed** | A compact, **generator-discovered** inventory: N plugins, and for the active plugin its agent/skill/hook/command counts. Discover at build time the same way `_commands_inventory()` and `_decision_trees_inventory()` already walk `plugins/*/…` — never hard-code counts (House Rule 1). | `PLUGINS_DIR.glob(...)` |
| **The big systems** | Four "system cards" with a one-line plain-language summary + a deep-link to the tab that controls it: **Comfort posture** → Settings; **Guardrail pipeline** → Pipeline; **Command-review tribunal (the Thing)** → Settings/Pipeline + Review log; **Install bridge** → Install. | `docs/architecture.md`, the pipeline lane definitions (`_PIPELINE_LANES` :206) |
| **Start here** | A 3-step checklist with deep-links: (1) Pick a posture preset → Settings; (2) See what the guardrails do → Pipeline; (3) Wire the plugin into your tool → Install. | — |
| **Served-state banner** | Reuse the existing `live` badge idiom: when served, show "Live — changes save to *this* repo"; when static, show "Preview — read-only; run `python3 scripts/serve-dashboards.py` to edit." This answers the most common confusion (the published GitHub Pages copy is read-only, per `README.md` line 5). | `HEAD /__save` probe |

Keep it **static-host-safe** (build-time embed, like the Guidance tab) so it renders
identically on GitHub Pages and served. The only live element is the served/static banner.

---

## 2. Per-tab audit

| Tab | Renderer | Current state | Interactive? | Gap | Recommendation |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **(none) Overview** | — | **Missing** | — | No purpose/contents/first-run framing (§1) | **P0** — add Overview tab, make it default |
| **Settings** | `_render_settings_tab` :903 | Posture editor: preset bar, per-category file/network/shell/package radios (deny→ask→allow) per layer, tribunal panel, security-deny. Round-trips `comfort-posture.yaml` via `/__save`/`/__read`. | **Yes** (served) | Loads first with zero framing; richest/most intimidating surface; the only entry point. | Demote to tab 2 (after Overview). Add a one-line "what this tab does" header. Otherwise strong — no content removal. |
| **Pipeline** | `_render_pipeline_tab` :361 | Visual map of all guardrail stages across `SessionStart → PreToolUse → PostToolUse → Stop`, with live ON/OFF badges, 5th-grade tooltips, and inline editors for tunable stages (`_PIPELINE_CONTROLS` :263). File-backed stages round-trip `.repo-layout.json` / `task-scope.json`. | **Yes** — the **best-realized** interactive tab | Discoverability: users may not realize the same knobs live in Settings (overlap — see §5). | Keep. Cross-link to Settings (already partially done via `<a href="#/settings">`). This is the model the other static tabs should aspire to. |
| **Install & Update** | `_render_install_tab` :887 | Copy-to-clipboard command blocks (`_INSTALL_COMMANDS` :626) + one-click Install/Update/Status buttons that POST `/__run` (served only; disabled until `HEAD /__run` succeeds, :4072). | **Partly** — 3 actions live when served, else copy | Solid pattern, but it is the **only** place real shell execution happens. The Commands tab does *not* reuse it (see §4). | Keep. Use it as the **reference design** for the executing-Commands work. |
| **Test a command** | `_render_simulator_tab` :150 | Type a shell command → POST `/__classify` → shows category/tier/seats/concerns/gate from the **real** engine (no execution). Disabled with help text when static. | **Yes** (served) | None major. Naming ("Test a command") is close to "Commands" and may confuse — see §5. | Keep. Consider clarifying label to "Preview a command's review" to disambiguate from Commands. |
| **Learn** | `_render_learn_tab` :564 | Concept cards from `concepts.json`: searchable, expandable categories, inline SVG diagrams, "try it" widgets, see-also chips, sources. | **Yes** (client-side; works offline) | Strong already. Decoupled from Guidance (which also explains priors) — mild overlap. | Keep. Cross-link Learn ↔ Guidance. |
| **⚖ Review log** | `_render_saga_tab` :173 | Live filterable table of tribunal verdicts via `GET /__saga`; verdict + category filters; degrades on static host. | **Yes** (served) | Empty/served state messaging is the only thing a static visitor sees. | Keep. From Overview, deep-link here so users discover the audit trail. |
| **Commands** | `_render_commands_tab` :728 | Card grid of every marketplace slash command (`_commands_inventory()` :684). **Each card is copy-to-clipboard only** — `_render_command_card` :706 emits a `/name` `<code>` + Copy button. The code comment (:670–679) explains why: "there is no live IPC to launch a command from a browser." | **No** — copy only | **Maintainer's explicit ask:** commands must be **clickable + execute + tooltips** (§4). | **P0** — redesign per §4. |
| **Guidance** | `_render_trees_tab` :820 | Build-time embed of every plugin's `## Decision Tree:` sections + `best-practices/*.md`, grouped per plugin in `<details>`, each item an `<a>` link to the **source `.md` file**. | **Partly** — expand/collapse `<details>`; links open raw files | Items only **link out** to a `.md`; the Mermaid in decision-tree docs is **not** rendered inline; best-practices show no preview. A user must open a raw markdown file to see anything. | **P1** — render Mermaid inline + preview-on-click (§3). |
| **Activity** | `_render_activity_tab` :183 | Newest-first feed of all `.ravenclaude/runs/<id>/` artifacts via `GET /__runs`; reuses Saga CSS; degrades on static host. | **Yes** (served) | Overlaps conceptually with Review log (verdicts vs. runs) — the distinction is real but under-explained. | Keep. Add a one-line "Review log = command verdicts; Activity = multi-step runs" disambiguator (the renderer docstring already states it; surface it in the UI). |

---

## 3. Interactivity recommendations (static content that should become interactive)

| Surface | Today | Should become | How (respecting static-host-safety + security) |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Guidance — decision trees** | `<a>` link to the raw `.md` source (`_render_trees_tab` :845–855). The Mermaid diagram inside lives only in the file. | **Expandable tree with the Mermaid rendered inline.** Click an item → it expands in place and draws the diagram. | The page already renders Mermaid elsewhere (decision trees ship as ```mermaid blocks). At **build time**, extract each ```` ```mermaid ```` block from the discovered doc and embed it in a collapsed `<details>` under the item; render with the same client-side Mermaid the Learn tab uses for its inline SVGs. No server needed → still works on GitHub Pages. Keep the "open source file" link as a secondary action. |
| **Guidance — best practices** | Link-only; status pill but no body. | **Preview-on-click.** Click → inline disclosure shows the doc's intro/summary (first heading + first paragraph, or the `**Status:**`/rationale block). | Embed a truncated preview at build time (mirror `_best_practices_inventory()` :791, which already parses the title + status). Full doc stays one click away. Static-safe. |
| **Learn ↔ Guidance** | Separate, no cross-nav. | Bidirectional chips. | Both are build-time inventories; add `see_also`-style chips linking a concept to the decision tree that operationalizes it. |
| **Overview "big systems"** | (new) | Deep-link cards (§1). | Static anchors (`#/settings`, `#/pipeline`, …) — same hash-routing the tabs already use. |
| **Commands** | Copy only. | **Click-to-execute + tooltip** (§4 — the headline ask). | See §4. |

**Pipeline is already the gold standard** of interactivity (live badges + inline editors +
file round-trip). The recommendation is to *bring the static tabs up to it*, not to change
Pipeline.

---

## 4. Commands must be **clickable + EXECUTE + tooltips** (hard requirement)

### 4.1 Current behavior (verified)

`_render_command_card()` ([:706](../scripts/generate-dashboards.py)) emits, per command,
a title (`/name`), an owner badge, the description, and a `/name` `<code>` block with a
**Copy** button — nothing executes. The Commands tab intro (:739) literally says "Copy a
command and paste it into Claude Code to run it." The code comment (:670–679) states the
constraint plainly: **"there is no live IPC to launch a command from a browser."**

The four shipped commands (`_commands_inventory()` discovers them from
`plugins/*/commands/*.md`) are all **Claude Code slash commands**, i.e. in-Claude prompts:

| Command | Owner | What it actually does | Executable from the dashboard server? |
|---|---|---|---|
| `/init-agent-ready` | ravenclaude-core | Guided in-Claude flow that writes `AGENTS.md` / `CLAUDE.md` / `.repo-layout.json` | **No** — it's a Claude Code prompt, not a shell command |
| `/dashboard` | ravenclaude-core | Launches the functional dashboard (`allowed-tools: Bash, Read`) | **In-Claude only** — but its *effect* (open the dashboard) is shell-shaped (`bash scripts/open-dashboard.sh`) |
| `/set-posture` | ravenclaude-core | Applies `comfort-posture.yaml` → `.claude/settings.json` (`allowed-tools: Bash, Read`) | **In-Claude only** — but its core effect maps to `thing-decide`/posture-apply shell, related to what `/__save`'s apply step already does |
| `/wrap` | ravenclaude-core | In-Claude flow that captures a lessons-learned scenario file | **No** — a Claude Code prompt |

### 4.2 Capability Grounding (the honest constraint)

A web page **cannot launch a Claude Code slash command.** Slash commands are interpreted by
the Claude Code CLI inside a running session; the browser has no IPC channel into that
session. The dashboard server's `/__run` endpoint can execute **only** the three fixed
allow-listed *shell* actions (`install` / `update` / `status`, `ALLOWED_ACTIONS`
[:83](../scripts/serve-dashboards.py)) — and only when the page is **served** by
`serve-dashboards.py` (CSRF-guarded, `127.0.0.1`-bound). So "execute every command on click"
is **not** literally achievable for slash commands without lying to the user. The design
below executes what is genuinely shell-executable and is honest about the rest.

### 4.3 Specified design — three execution classes, one card component

Every command card becomes a **button**, with a **tooltip/disclosure that shows exactly what
it runs** (verbatim argv or the slash string). Classify each command at build time into one
of three execution classes, and render the primary action accordingly:

| Class | Definition | Primary button | Tooltip / disclosure (required for all) | Served vs. static |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **A — shell-executable, allow-listed** | The command maps to an existing `ALLOWED_ACTIONS` entry (or a newly allow-listed one — see 4.4). | **▶ Run** → POST `/__run {"action": …}`, reusing the exact Install-tab mechanic (`data-run-action`, the `HEAD /__run` probe, the spinner + `run-result` panel at :4072–4083). | Shows the **fixed argv** that will run, e.g. `bash scripts/ravenclaude status --project <root>`. | Button **enabled when served**; disabled + "run `serve-dashboards.py`" help when static (identical to Install tab). |
| **B — in-Claude slash command** | A Claude Code prompt command (`/init-agent-ready`, `/wrap`, and the in-Claude parts of `/dashboard`, `/set-posture`). | **Copy** (primary) + a small **"Runs inside Claude Code"** pill. Optionally a **"Send to Claude"** deep-link *only if* a desktop handler is known (do not assume one — the original comment at :679 flags the deep-link tier as needing an unavailable desktop handler). | Tooltip states: "This is a Claude Code slash command. It runs inside your Claude session, not from this page. Click Copy, then paste `/name` into Claude Code." Show the exact `/name`. | Copy works on **every** host (file:// / Pages / served). |
| **C — shell-effect of a slash command** | A slash command whose *practical effect* is a fixed shell action we already trust (e.g. `/dashboard` ≈ `bash scripts/open-dashboard.sh`; `/set-posture` ≈ the posture-apply that `/__save` already triggers). | Offer **both**: a **▶ Run the shell effect** button (Class-A path, only if the effect is added to the allow-list per 4.4) **and** Copy of the `/name`. | Tooltip distinguishes them: "Run here executes `bash scripts/open-dashboard.sh`. Pasting `/dashboard` into Claude does the same thing inside your session." | Run button served-only; Copy everywhere. |

**Component-level requirements (apply to every card, every class):**

1. The card root is a real control (button or has an explicit `▶ Run` / `Copy` action), not just a `<code>` + copy.
2. **Tooltip/disclosure is mandatory and shows the exact thing executed** — the literal argv for Class A/C, the literal `/name` for Class B. Use a `title=`/`aria-describedby` tooltip plus an always-visible "what this runs" line so it's not hover-only (accessibility + touch).
3. Reuse the **served-state idiom**: Run buttons start `disabled`, enable on the `HEAD /__run` probe, show the spinner + result panel on click — do not invent a second execution path; extend the Install tab's.
4. **Copy never goes away.** It is the universal fallback and the only mechanism for Class B. (Matches the existing "Copy works on every host" guarantee.)
5. Result/exit-code surfaced inline (reuse `run-result` / `run-result-badge` / `run-result-output`, :4077).

### 4.4 Should `/__run`'s allow-list be widened? — yes, narrowly; with guardrails

**Recommendation: widen the allow-list to a small set of *named, fixed-argv* actions, never
to arbitrary commands.** Today only `install`/`update`/`status` are runnable. To make
Class-A/C commands genuinely clickable, add **specific, audited** actions, e.g.:

| Proposed new action | Fixed argv | Why it's safe to allow-list |
|---|---|---|
| `open-dashboard` | `bash scripts/open-dashboard.sh` | Already the documented one-command launcher (`README.md` line 7); no user input. |
| `set-posture` (apply) | the same posture-apply already invoked by `/__save`'s apply step (`_handle_save` apply path, serve-dashboards.py ~:589–610) | The apply is already reachable via Save; exposing it as a named button changes the entry point, not the capability. |

**Security envelope that must hold for any widening (non-negotiable):**

- It stays a **closed `set()` of action *names*** validated with `action not in ALLOWED_ACTIONS → 403` (`_handle_run` :627). **No caller-supplied args, no shell, no interpolation** beyond the validated name (preserve the fixed-`argv` construction at :635).
- Each new action maps to **one fixed argv** reviewed in code, exactly like `install`/`status` append `--project <root>` and nothing else.
- **CSRF + bind posture unchanged**: Origin/Host guard (`do_POST` :522) and `127.0.0.1` bind stay. Served-only; never reachable from a static host or cross-origin page.
- **High-blast actions never go on the list.** Anything destructive/irreversible (deletes, force-push, prod) is out of scope for `/__run` — consistent with the tribunal's high-blast→defer rule in `CLAUDE.md`. Those remain Class B (copy + run-in-Claude) so a human is in the loop.
- Document each addition next to `ALLOWED_ACTIONS` with a one-line justification, so the allow-list is self-auditing.

**Net:** widen by a couple of audited, fixed-argv, non-destructive actions to honor
"clickable + execute"; keep slash-command-only flows as Class B with an honest tooltip +
copy. This satisfies the maintainer's ask *as far as the platform allows* without ever
opening an arbitrary-command hole.

---

## 5. Consolidate + add — **never remove**

No tab or content should be deleted. The overlaps below are **consolidation/cross-linking**
opportunities, not removals:

| Overlap | What overlaps | Consolidation (additive only) |
|---|---|---|
| **Settings ↔ Pipeline** | The tribunal/runaway/decision/dod/file knobs appear in both (Pipeline's `_PIPELINE_CONTROLS` :263 deep-link to Settings; Settings owns the full editor). | Keep both. Make Pipeline the **"see what each guardrail does"** view and Settings the **"tune everything"** view; cross-link explicitly (Pipeline already links to `#/settings`). State the division of labor in each tab header. |
| **Review log ↔ Activity** | Both are newest-first feeds; Saga = tribunal verdicts, Activity = all runs (Activity explicitly skips the thing/ dir — docstring :486). | Keep both. Add a one-line disambiguator in each UI ("verdicts" vs. "multi-step runs"). Optionally surface both from the Overview as "Audit & history." |
| **Learn ↔ Guidance** | Both explain priors/concepts; Learn = curated concept cards, Guidance = discovered decision trees + best-practices. | Keep both. Cross-link concept ↔ decision-tree. |
| **Test a command ↔ Commands** | Adjacent labels; different jobs (simulate review vs. list/run commands). | Keep both. Relabel "Test a command" → "Preview a command's review" to remove the name collision. |

**Explicit constraint honored:** this audit proposes **adding** an Overview tab,
**upgrading** Commands and Guidance to be interactive, and **cross-linking** overlaps. It
proposes **removing nothing.**

---

## 6. Prioritized recommendations

### P0 — the maintainer's explicit asks

| # | Recommendation | Where it lands |
|---|---|---|
| P0-1 | **Commands become clickable + execute + tooltips.** Class A/C commands run via `/__run` (served), Class B (slash-only) get an honest "runs inside Claude Code" tooltip + Copy. Every card shows *exactly what it executes* (argv or `/name`). | `_render_command_card` / `_render_commands_tab` (:706/:728); reuse Install tab's `/__run` mechanic (:4072) |
| P0-2 | **Widen `/__run` allow-list narrowly** to a few audited, fixed-argv, non-destructive actions (`open-dashboard`, posture-apply) so Class-A/C commands genuinely run. **Stays a closed allow-list; CSRF + 127.0.0.1 + no-shell preserved; high-blast excluded.** | `ALLOWED_ACTIONS` (:83), `_handle_run` (:612) |
| P0-3 | **Add an Overview tab and make it the default.** Communicate what RavenClaude is, what's installed (generator-discovered), the four big systems, a "start here" checklist, and a served/static banner. | new `_render_overview_tab()`; register first in tab bar (:6742); move `active` off Settings |
| P0-4 | **Make static guidance interactive.** Guidance: render decision-tree Mermaid inline + best-practice preview-on-click (build-time embed; static-safe). | `_render_trees_tab` (:820), `_decision_trees_inventory` (:764), `_best_practices_inventory` (:791) |

### P1 — high-value, lower-risk

| # | Recommendation |
|---|---|
| P1-1 | Add a one-line "what this tab does" header to Settings, Review log, Activity (purpose framing inside each tab). |
| P1-2 | Relabel "Test a command" → "Preview a command's review" to remove the Commands/Test-a-command name collision. |
| P1-3 | Cross-link the overlapping pairs (Settings↔Pipeline, Review log↔Activity, Learn↔Guidance) with explicit "division of labor" text — consolidation without removal. |
| P1-4 | Surface the served/static state consistently across all tabs (one shared banner component), reusing the existing `live` badge idiom. |

### P2 — polish

| # | Recommendation |
|---|---|
| P2-1 | From Overview, deep-link the "audit & history" surfaces (Review log + Activity) so the tribunal's auditability is discoverable. |
| P2-2 | Concept ↔ decision-tree "see also" chips between Learn and Guidance. |
| P2-3 | Optional "Send to Claude" deep-link for Class-B commands **only if** a desktop handler is confirmed available (do not assume one — the original code comment flags it as unavailable). |

---

## 7. Constraints this spec deliberately respects

- **Security envelope unchanged.** Allow-list-only `/__run`, fixed argv, no shell, CSRF Origin/Host guard, `127.0.0.1` bind, served-only execution, high-blast excluded.
- **House Rule 1 (domain-neutral core).** Every inventory (Overview counts, Commands, Guidance) is **generator-discovered** from `plugins/*/…` at build time — never hard-coded domain names, matching `_commands_inventory()` / `_decision_trees_inventory()`.
- **Static-host parity.** GitHub Pages / `file://` must keep working; new content is build-time-embedded or degrades gracefully, exactly like the existing tabs.
- **Additive only.** No content removed; overlaps consolidated via cross-linking.
- **Honesty over magic.** The browser-cannot-launch-a-slash-command limit is stated to the user in the Commands tooltips, not papered over.
